title: Foxconn and Intel Just Bet Everything on Building AI's Hardware Backbone
script: The world's biggest contract manufacturer and one of its top chipmakers just agreed to build the future of AI together. Foxconn and Intel announced June 4 they will co-develop AI server racks, robotics platforms, and edge computing systems. Intel brings the chips; Foxconn brings the factories. Intel stock jumped 4.4% to $112 on the news. The real war in AI isn't just about models — it's about who controls the hardware.
